constraints_1_content: Before writing /tmp/solution, use Bash to explore the /app/data directory structure: `find /app/data -maxdepth 3 -ls 2>/dev/null | head -80` to understand file distribution, hidden entries, and symlink presence.
constraints_1_rationale: This is a task-specific tool usage directive telling the agent to inspect the data tree first; it references ClaudeCode's Bash tool.
constraints_2_content: After creating /tmp/solution, validate it by running `bash -n /tmp/solution` via Bash to catch syntax errors before any functional execution.
constraints_2_rationale: Specific to the deliverable script; uses ClaudeCode Bash tool for syntax checking.
constraints_3_content: Use `Write` to create /tmp/solution as a new file; do not use `Edit` on a file that does not yet exist.
constraints_3_rationale: Directly references ClaudeCode Write vs Edit tools in the context of creating the deliverable script.
constraints_4_content: NOTE: Do not use `rm -rf` or any destructive commands targeting directories outside /tmp or /app/output. The script must never delete original files under /app/data.
constraints_4_rationale: Task-dependent because it guards /app/data integrity; framework-independent safety policy.
constraints_5_content: Sort the CSV output rows by file path in LC_ALL=C lexicographic order to guarantee deterministic byte-for-byte reproducibility across runs.
constraints_5_rationale: Task-dependent because it specifies how to achieve determinism for this specific report; framework-independent implementation detail.
constraints_7_content: Add inline comments in the script for each major section (file discovery, pattern matching & replacement, CSV generation) so the logic is auditable without re-reading the full task spec.
constraints_7_rationale: Task-dependent as it references the specific sections of this script; framework-independent code documentation practice.
